commands.purpose: Enumerate Regions. Filter by tenant / status / country_code / is_default. Prints a table with id, tenant, name, country_code, currency, timezone, status, is_default, sort_order. Ops tooling only.

Print the resolution trace for one Region: full row + resolved defaults from country-defaults.json (side-by-side, so overrides are visible) + inbound reference counts (Branch, Membership, Invoice, Transaction) + last 10 audit rows when --include-audit-history. --include-tax-config expands the tax_config blob (requires the operator's account to hold regions.view.tax_registration).

CLI create with country-defaults auto-fill. Only `tenant` + `country_code` are required — every other field defaults from `data/country-defaults.json`. --default sets is_default=true (auto-demotes the previous default). Consumes 1 region_slot entitlement unless --system is set (reserved for the migration path).
Flip is_default=true on the given Region + auto-demote the previous default in same transaction. Fires RegionDefaultChanged. Refused when the Region's status is not active.
Set status=paused + fire RegionPaused. --resume-at accepts an ISO 8601 timestamp; the scheduler auto-resumes at the boundary. Refused when is_default=true.
Set status=active + fire RegionResumed. Reverse of pause.

Soft-archive the Region + fire RegionArchived. Refused when is_default=true. Refused when any inbound row references this Region (unless --force is set + operator confirms; --force bypasses the FK check but does NOT delete referring rows — it leaves them pointing at a now-archived Region, which they can survive for 730 days).

Force-run the ReconcileDefaultRegionInvariantJob. --tenant scopes to a single tenant; without it, scans every tenant. --auto-heal enables the healing steps (promote-oldest OR re-seed-from-country_code); without it, the command just reports drift. --dry-run reports the planned actions without persisting.
Dump the tax_config blob for compliance review. --include-history reads the last N days of RegionTaxConfigUpdated audit rows + prints the diff sequence. Handy during pre-invoice financial audits.

One-shot: at Wave 2a deploy time, iterate every existing tenant that lacks a default Region + dispatch SeedDefaultRegionForTenantJob for each. --limit chunks the run to avoid dispatching thousands of jobs at once. --dry-run reports the target list without dispatching. Idempotent — re-running is safe.
